task_completion.description: Open the TopoCal application and attempt to load the specified DXF file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: TopoCal application window is visible. The agent opens the file explorer or open dialog to load 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\building_design.dxf' (Note: the file may be missing due to a task defect, so look for the attempt).

task_completion.description: Locate and select the building foundation polyline or the relevant tool for vertex extraction.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent selects a polyline in the TopoCal workspace or navigates to the specific menu/tool used for interacting with polylines and vertices.

task_completion.description: Convert the polyline's existing vertices into survey points without interpolating or dividing the segments.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog, menu option, or command in TopoCal is visible for converting a polyline to points. Settings or checkboxes should indicate that interpolation, division, or adding extra points is disabled.

task_completion.description: Export the resulting survey points to the specified CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export or save dialog in TopoCal is open, showing the file format set to CSV and the destination path explicitly typed or selected as 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\stakeout_points.csv'.

task_completion.description: Save the TopoCal workspace to the specified .top file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog in TopoCal is open, showing the destination path explicitly typed or selected as 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\building_stakeout.top'.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the TopoCal graphical user interface to perform the tasks rather than terminal commands or scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent clicking through TopoCal menus, ribbons, and dialog boxes. There should be no evidence of Python scripts or PowerShell commands being used to bypass the application.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not manually type the expected coordinates into a text editor to fake the CSV output.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like Notepad or VS Code) should be visible with the agent manually typing out the 8 expected vertex coordinates.

integrity.description: Verify that the CSV and TOP files were created via TopoCal's native save/export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: Standard Windows save/export dialogs must be triggered directly from within the TopoCal application, rather than files being created via terminal echo commands or file explorers.
